markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Compute backlinks from explicit links, mentions, tags, citations, graph edges, and unlinked mentions with evidence. This MT belongs to LoomObsidianNavigation. Deliver the Obsidian replacement surface as a Handshake-native Loom projection.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Obsidian-like behavior must be implemented as Loom/project knowledge projections over PostgreSQL, not a markdown-vault clone.
Backlinks, graph, tags, folders, pins, color labels, and project links must have backend navigation APIs and visual-debug evidence.
scope.expected_behavior: BacklinkComputation leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: BacklinkComputation is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: Linked backlinks (with surrounding-text context snippets) + unlinked-mention scanner over loom_edges + loom_blocks Postgres store. No parallel index; reuses the existing get_backlinks incoming-edge query.
files_changed: src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/loom.rs - LoomBacklink, LoomUnlinkedMention types; pure helpers loom_context_snippet (char-safe windowed excerpt w/ ellipses, LOOM_SNIPPET_CONTEXT_CHARS=48) + loom_find_unlinked_term (case-insensitive word-boundary match returning char offset); 5 unit tests (mt178_helper_tests)
src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs - PostgresDatabase impls + loom_block_scan_text helper (title + derived full_text_index)

evidence.command: cargo test -p handshake_core --features runtime-full,test-utils --lib storage::loom::mt178_helper_tests  &&  --test loom_backlink_tests -- --test-threads=1
evidence.result: unit ok. 5 passed; integration ok. 4 passed; 0 failed (real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL)
tests: PG linked_backlinks_carry_source_block_and_context_snippet - incoming MENTION edge -> source block + snippet containing the reference
PG unlinked_mentions_surface_only_unlinked_word_boundary_matches - unlinked 'Roadmap' surfaces; 'Roadmapping' (substring) excluded; already-linked block excluded; matched_term+snippet+offset present
PG unlinked_mentions_scan_aliases_too - alias term ('Plan-of-Record') is scanned
PG backlinks_fail_closed_on_missing_block - NotFound, never silent success

evidence: Status flip note (2026-06-12T06:25:27.785Z): Loom engine group backend-complete per delegate report - real-PG proven (isolated schemas, full migration chain), per-MT adversarial review recorded in this file. SQLite removed/unreachable (orphans git-rm'd, sqlx has no sqlite feature, source regression guard added). Markdown-vault-never-authority proven via projection-delete byte-identity negative test. Stable /workspaces/:ws/loom/* HTTP contracts documented for the Notes UI. Implementation commit(s): 991985b9.

findings: ILIKE-INJECTION (clear): scan prefilter uses escape_like_token + sqlx parameterized binds; no string interpolation of the term into SQL.
DOS (mitigated): scan_unlinked_mentions bounds rows via limit.clamp(1,500) + an ILIKE prefilter before the O(text*terms) Rust word-boundary pass; backlinks are bounded by the incoming-edge count.
SELF-MENTION (handled): the scan excludes b.block_id = the viewed block, so a block mentioning its own title is not reported.
ALREADY-LINKED EXCLUSION (handled+tested): NOT EXISTS guard removes blocks that already have a mention/tag/sub_tag edge to the target, so 'unlinked' means truly unlinked.
WORD-BOUNDARY CORRECTNESS (handled+tested): loom_find_unlinked_term rejects alphanumeric-flanked matches (Roadmapping != Roadmap); snippet operates on chars not bytes (no multibyte split).

NON-ASCII-LOWERCASE (minor, accepted): the case-insensitive match uses an ASCII-lowercase fast path with a Unicode-lowercase fallback only when lengths differ; a few non-Latin uppercase forms (e.g. Turkish dotted I) could miss. No security impact; common Latin/UTF-8 text matches correctly.
N+1 (accepted): get_backlinks_with_context loads each source block individually; bounded by the backlink count (panel-scale). Acceptable; can batch later if a hot path emerges.
fixes: No code change required beyond the implemented guards; all findings are either handled-by-design (and tested) or accepted low-impact with a rationale.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
